mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate above 6% over a 3-month period signals a weakening labor market that compresses corporate profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with UNRATE above 6% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing starts above 900k units over a 2-month period signals an improving demand environment that boosts corporate sales growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in periods with HOUST above 900k are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in industrial production below 110 index points over a 2-month period signals a weakening supply environment that compresses corporate profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with INDPRO below 110 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate above 4.5% over a 3-month period signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in the unemployment rate below 5% over a 2-month period signals an improving labor market that boosts corporate profit margins, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with UNRATE below 5% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in real GDP above $12 trillion over a 6-month period signals an improving demand environment that boosts corporate sales growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in periods with GDPC1 above $12 trillion are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in industrial production below 105 index points over a 2-month period signals a weakening supply environment that compresses corporate profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in periods with INDPRO below 105 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
